step_by_step_thinking: Acid etching of dentin involves exposing collagen fibers and openning dentinal tubules. It helps improve wetting and thus enhances adhesion of subsequent layers. However, acid etching does NOT specifically aim to increase surface tension; rather, it prepares the dentin surface for optimal bonding. Therefore, option D about increasing surface tension is incorrect regarding the purpose and effect of acid etching.
raw_response:
Acid etching of dentin involves exposing collagen fibers and openning dentinal tubules. It helps improve wetting and thus enhances adhesion of subsequent layers. However, acid etching does NOT specifically aim to increase surface tension; rather, it prepares the dentin surface for optimal bonding. Therefore, option D about increasing surface tension is incorrect regarding the purpose and effect of acid etching.
\boxed{D}
